answer:
\boxed{A}

The coat of arms of the King of Spain includes the arms of **Catalonia** alongside those of Castile, León, and Aragon, reflecting its historical union with Spain through the Crown of Aragon. Navarre was also part of the Crown of Aragon but is not prominently featured in the royal coat of arms today. Granada was the last Muslim kingdom in Iberia, conquered in 1492, but its arms are not included in the monarch’s heraldry.
